top_stories.title: ServiceNow discloses security incident exposing customer data
top_stories.summary: ServiceNow disclosed a security incident in which attackers exploited an unauthenticated access vulnerability through an API endpoint to query data from customer instances. The breach involved an access control flaw that allowed unauthorized parties to retrieve customer data without authentication.

top_stories.title: Critical Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited to Bypass Passwords in IKEv1 Setups
top_stories.summary: Check Point disclosed active exploitation of CVE-2026-50751, a critical (CVSS 9.3) authentication bypass vulnerability in its Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access products using the deprecated IKEv1 protocol. The flaw is a logic weakness in certificate validation that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to bypass password authentication.

top_stories.title: Silverfort Integrates AI Agent Identity Control for Microsoft Copilot Studio
top_stories.summary: Silverfort announced an integration with Microsoft Copilot Studio that applies runtime identity and access controls to AI agents. The integration aims to extend Silverfort's identity security capabilities to agentic AI workloads, addressing the emerging challenge of non-human identity governance.

top_stories.title: Silverfort brings runtime identity controls to Microsoft Copilot Studio agents
top_stories.summary: Silverfort has launched an integration that applies its identity and access controls to AI agents built in Microsoft Copilot Studio, enforcing policy at runtime when an agent attempts to take action. The integration extends Silverfort's identity security controls to non-human identities in the form of AI agents, addressing access governance for automated AI workflows.

top_stories.title: French government messaging platform breached through account hijacking
top_stories.summary: The French government's secure messaging platform Tchap was breached after hackers hijacked a user account to gain access to public chat rooms. The platform, built on the open-source Matrix protocol and used by civil servants and public agencies, is under investigation by the Interministerial Directorate for Digital Affairs (DINUM).

news.title: Hades PyPI Attack: 19 Packages Poisoned to Auto-Run Bun Credential Stealer
news.summary: A supply chain attack called Hades has been discovered on PyPI, involving 37 malicious wheel artifacts across 19 packages designed to automatically execute a Bun-based credential stealer. The attack is part of the broader Miasma campaign and targets credential theft through poisoned Python packages.

news.title: 株式会社S-FITの認証基盤統合とセキュリティ強化を支援した事例を公開
news.summary: SoftCreate published a case study about supporting S-FIT Co., Ltd. in consolidating their authentication infrastructure and strengthening security. The article appears to detail an IAM/authentication integration project, though specific technical details are limited in the provided description.

news.title: Top identity and access management risks
news.summary: The article addresses the evolving landscape of identity and access management risks, covering SaaS applications, AI agents, and machine-to-machine interactions in distributed environments. It focuses on the expanded scope of modern IAM challenges beyond traditional user authentication.

news.title: UNC3753 Used Vishing and Physical Intrusions in U.S. Data Theft Extortion Campaign
news.summary: Google Mandiant and Google Threat Intelligence Group have attributed a data theft extortion campaign targeting U.S. organizations in professional, legal, and financial services to threat actor UNC3753, active between January and May 2026. The campaign employed vishing (voice phishing) and physical intrusions as attack vectors to compromise organizations and steal data.

news.title: AI Agent Governance: Managing Permissions, Auditing, and Compliance
news.summary: The article covers AI agent governance in enterprise .NET applications, focusing on permissions, role-based access management, auditing, and compliance controls. The content addresses how organizations can manage authorization and access oversight for AI agents, which falls within identity governance and access management principles.

news.title: SingleID、Microsoft Entra IDとのSCIM連携を提供開始
news.summary: SingleID has launched SCIM integration support with Microsoft Entra ID. This integration enables automated user provisioning and synchronization between SingleID and Microsoft Entra ID.

news.title: Building secure B2C applications with fine-grained access control using Amazon Cognito and Amazon Verified Permissions
news.summary: Amazon has published guidance on building secure B2C applications using Amazon Cognito for authentication and Amazon Verified Permissions for fine-grained access control. The article covers implementing authentication and authorization controls together as core security pillars for modern web applications.

news.title: Willow raises $7M to build the identity layer for autonomous AI agents
news.summary: Willow has raised $7M to develop an identity layer specifically designed for autonomous AI agents. The funding targets a emerging gap in identity management infrastructure as enterprises increasingly deploy agentic AI systems that require their own authentication and authorization frameworks.

news.title: The AWS MCP Server now supports cross-account and cross-role access
news.summary: AWS has added cross-account and cross-role access capabilities to the AWS Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server within its Agent Toolkit. The feature enables developers using AI coding agents to operate across multiple AWS accounts and IAM roles without manually reconfiguring credentials.

news.title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
news.summary: A coding error in Microsoft 365 Android apps left a security setting for authentication protection disabled, exposing accounts to potential takeover. The vulnerability affected key apps including Word, PowerPoint, and Excel, allowing attackers to steal login credentials and data.

news.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
news.summary: A phishing-as-a-service platform called Kali365, previously flagged by the FBI, has expanded its targeting beyond Microsoft 365 to include AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms. The platform relies on device code phishing techniques to compromise identity provider credentials and cloud service accounts.

news.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
news.summary: A security researcher published multiple zero-day exploits, prompting Microsoft to suggest potential criminal charges against the individual. The situation has generated backlash within the security research community regarding Microsoft's legal response to vulnerability disclosure.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to bypass account security and reset Instagram passwords, enabling account takeovers. The attack affected high-profile accounts including the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official, with attack instructions distributed via Telegram.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a significant credential leak. Lawmakers from both congressional chambers are demanding answers while CISA works to contain the breach and invalidate the exposed credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system access details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked files reportedly included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, exposing multiple privileged accounts and internal systems.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. His conviction relates to SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in theft of tens of millions in cryptocurrency.
